zh.lifeSummary: 當Chamoa Fernández de Acevedo於1230年出生在西班牙卡斯利亞-拉 曼哈托萊多時，她的父親Fernández Pérez Portugales29歲，母親Maria de Aceves de Toledo40歲。她於大約1252年與Pedro Gomez de Barroso在西班牙劍橋大學默里 卡斯利結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她去世於1258年享年28歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Chamoa Fernández de Acevedo于1230年出生在西班牙卡斯利亞-拉 曼哈托萊多时，她的父亲Fernández Pérez Portugales29岁，母亲Maria de Aceves de Toledo40岁。她于大约1252年与Pedro Gomez de Barroso在西班牙劍橋大學默里 卡斯利结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和5个女儿的父母。她去世于1258年享年28岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Acevedo
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Luis, Carlos, Manuel, Miguel, Jesus, Francisco, Jorge, Angel, Raul, Rafael.  Portuguese Wenceslao.

Spanish, Galician, and Portuguese: topographic name for someone whose dwelling was by a clump of holly bushes, from medieval acebedo, azevedo ‘holly grove’ (from azevo ‘holly’ + -edo ‘plantation’). Compare Acebedo , Asebedo , Asevedo , and Azevedo .

variants.name: Acebedo
meanings: Spanish and Galician: variant of   Acevedo  . This surname is also found in the Philippines. Compare   Asebedo  .

variants.name: Azevedo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Manuel, Jose, Mario, Fernando, Carlos, Alberto, Luis, Adelino, Alvarino, Alvaro, Anselmo, Claudio.  Portuguese Joao, Paulo, Agostinho, Joaquim, Aderito, Albano, Damiao, Duarte, Guilherme, Henrique, Ligia, Marcio.
Portuguese: topographic name for someone whose dwelling was by a clump of holly bushes (see   Acevedo  ). Compare   Asevedo  .

Jewish (Sephardic): adoption of the Portuguese surname (see 1 above) at the moment of conversion to Roman Catholicism. After the return to Judaism (generations later), some descendants retained the name their families used as Catholics.

nameMeanings.name: Chamoa
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized form of Slovenian Čampa (see Campa ).
Indian: unexplained.

variants.name: Campa
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Jesus, Jorge, Juan, Manuel, Marcelino, Adriana, Andres, Blanca, Fernando, Francisco.
Spanish, Galician, and Asturian-Leonese: habitational name from any of various places called with campa ‘ditch’, for example Campa in Lugo and Biscay provinces, or La Campa in Asturias.
Slovenian (Čampa): nickname for a clumsy, sluggish person, from čampa ‘fat hen’.

nameMeanings.name: Fernández
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Juan, Carlos, Luis, Jorge, Miguel, Fernando, Mario, Jesus, Pedro, Francisco.  Portuguese Ligia, Joao, Armanda, Godofredo, Wenceslao.

Spanish (Fernández): patronymic from the personal name Fernando . The surname (and to a lesser extent the variant Hernandez ) has also been established in southern Italy, mainly in Naples and Palermo, since the period of Spanish dominance there.

Jewish (Sephardic): adoption of the name in 1 above at the moment of conversion to Roman Catholicism. After the return to Judaism (generations later), some Jewish descendants retained the surname that their families had adopted as Roman Catholics.

variants.name: Fernando
meanings: A modern form of the old Spanish name Ferdinando, which is of Germanic (Visigothic) origin, from farð ‘journey’ (or possibly an altered form of frið ‘peace’) + nand ‘ready, prepared’. The name was hereditary in the royal families of Spain from an early date. It was borne by Ferdinand I ( d. 1065 ) of Castile and Leon, who conducted successful campaigns against the Moors, and by his descendant Ferdinand V ( 1452–1516 ), who finally expelled the Moors from Spain altogether; he also gave financial backing to Columbus. Through the marriage in 1496 of his daughter Joan the Mad of Castile to the Habsburg Archduke Philip , the name Ferdinand also became hereditary in the Austrian imperial family. Their younger son, Ferdinand ( 1503–64 ), acquired the succession to the kingdoms of Hungary and Bohemia by marriage in 1521 , and became Holy Roman Emperor in 1558 . See Ferdinand .

en.lifeSummary: When Chamoa Fernández de Acevedo was born in 1230, in Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Spain, her father, Fernández Pérez Portugales, was 29 and her mother, Maria de Aceves de Toledo, was 40. She married Pedro Gomez de Barroso about 1252, in New Castile, Spain. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 5 daughters. She died in 1258, at the age of 28.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Chamoa Fernández de Acevedo est née en 1230, à Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Espagne, son père, Fernández Pérez Portugales, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Maria de Aceves de Toledo, avait 40 ans. Elle a épousé Pedro Gomez de Barroso environ 1252, à New Castile, Espagne. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 5 filles. Elle est décédée en 1258, à l’âge de 28 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Chamoa Fernández de Acevedo 1230 in Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Spanien geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Fernández Pérez Portugales 29 und ihre Mutter Maria de Aceves de Toledo 40. Sie hat um 1252 in New Castile, Spanien Pedro Gomez de Barroso geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Sie starb 1258 im Alter von 28 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Chamoa Fernández de Acevedo è nata il nel 1230 nel Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Spagna, sua padre, Fernández Pérez Portugales, aveva 29 anni e sua madre, Maria de Aceves de Toledo, aveva 40 anni. Lei sposato Pedro Gomez de Barroso circa 1252, nel New Castile, Spagna. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 5 figlie. Morì nel 1258, all'età di 28 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Chamoa Fernández de Acevedoが1230年にスペインカスティラ-ラ マンチャトーレドで生まれた時，父Fernández Pérez Portugalesは29歳，母Maria de Aceves de Toledoは40歳であった。約1252年にスペインニュー カスティルでPedro Gomez de Barrosoと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼女は1258年に28歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Chamoa Fernández de Acevedo(이)가 1230년 스페인 카스틸라-라 먼차 톨도에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Fernández Pérez Portugales(은)는 29세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Maria de Aceves de Toledo(은)는 40세였다. 그녀는 대략 1252년 스페인 뉴 카스틸에서 Pedro Gomez de Barroso(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1258년 28세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Chamoa Fernández de Acevedo nasceu em 1230, em Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Espanha, seu pai, Fernández Pérez Portugales, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Maria de Aceves de Toledo, tinha 40 anos. Ela casou-se com Pedro Gomez de Barroso aproximadamente 1252, em New Castile, Espanha. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 5 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1258, com 28 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Chamoa Fernández de Acevedo родилась в 1230 г. в местности толедо, кастилла-ла манча, Испания, её отцу по имени Fernández Pérez Portugales было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Maria de Aceves de Toledo, 40. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Pedro Gomez de Barroso около 1252 г. в местности ню кастиль, Испания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 5 дочерей. Она умерла в 1258 г. в возрасте 28 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Chamoa Fernández de Acevedo nació en 1230, en Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, España, su papá, Fernández Pérez Portugales, tenía 29 años y su mamá, Maria de Aceves de Toledo, tenía 40 años. Se casó con Pedro Gomez de Barroso aproximadamente 1252, en New Castile, España. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió en 1258, a la edad de 28 años.
